description:
Currently lifecycle_executor.rs advances phases by incrementing current_phase_index (line ~243: let next_idx = workflow.current_phase_index + 1). When a Rework decision is issued, it re-runs the current phase at the same index. The target_phase field in WorkflowDecisionRecord exists but is ignored by the executor.

Changes needed:
1. In lifecycle_executor.rs apply_advance(): look up next phase by ID from pipeline config rather than index+1
2. In lifecycle_executor.rs apply_rework(): when target_phase is Some(id), find that phase in the phases vec by ID and set current_phase_index to its position
3. Add lookup helper: fn find_phase_index(phases: &[WorkflowPhaseExecution], phase_id: &str) -> Option<usize>
4. Update phase advancement in daemon_phase_pool.rs to use the new ID-based routing
5. Add tests: rework to a prior phase by ID, rework to current phase (backward compat), advance to specific phase

Key files:
- crates/orchestrator-core/src/workflow/lifecycle_executor.rs (lines 194-377)
- crates/orchestrator-cli/src/services/runtime/runtime_daemon/daemon_phase_pool.rs
- crates/orchestrator-core/src/state_machines/schema.rs (may need new transitions)
title: Replace index-based phase advancement with ID-based routing in lifecycle_executor
